author: codex
rationale: The repo cut over in place and removed the handoff-plus-mixed-log model, so one current-state path is now the only supported memory workflow.
statement: Use only the v2 memory tree: always-loaded briefs, subsystem packs, and typed history.

author: codex
rationale: DECISION_PLAN.md was tightened into an operating decision record, and the roadmap, README, and architecture pack were aligned so future sessions stop treating mixed LBA2-shaped runtime seams as already-generic engine core.
statement: Keep near-term execution parity-first and evidence-first for LBA2, but only call code engine core after explicit extraction satisfies the promotion rules in DECISION_PLAN.md.

author: codex
rationale: The tracked decision record now lives under sidequest/, so current-state docs need one canonical location instead of split root-versus-sidequest framing ownership.
statement: Treat sidequest/DECISION_PLAN.md as the canonical framing doc; roadmap, README, and memory docs should reference that path when describing parity-first execution and engine-core promotion rules.

author: codex
rationale: Memory consolidation reduced source-of-truth overlap: the roadmap, current focus, subsystem packs, and port README now carry canonical path state directly, while sidequest/ and LM_TASKS/ stay independent until explicitly promoted.
statement: Treat docs/LBA2_ZIG_PORT_PLAN.md, docs/codex_memory/current_focus.md, and relevant subsystem packs as canonical current-state framing; keep sidequest/ and LM_TASKS/ out of default pickup.

author: codex
rationale: Canonical sessions should not absorb sidequest and LM/debug noise by default, but future investigations still need a first-class path to recover excluded records without reading JSONL by hand.
statement: Keep noncanonical durable history out of default context pickup, but allow explicit retrieval when a task asks for it.
